title: Grammar and spelling issues in lex.zig

content.text: Scan all content of @src/lex.zig and list out all wrong grammar, spelling in English in this file. Don't change the file.

content.text:
English issues found in `src/lex.zig` (file not changed):

- L552: `expect Span.to_utf16 produce...` → `produces`.
- L585: `a range of character` → `a range of characters`.
- L606-L607: `we will skip processing if the buffer longer than 15` → `...if the buffer is longer than 15`.
- L635: `in range null or 0 -> 15` → `in the range null or 0 -> 15`.
- L645: `could be equal or more than buffer_effective` → `could be equal to or greater than buffer_effective`.
- L820, L925, L1118, L1182, L1388: `appliceable` → `applicable`; sentence should be `...but that doesn't mean it is applicable for all cases.`
- L1284, L1442: `stop process Vietnamese input` → `stop processing Vietnamese input`.
- L1494: `inbound of the buffer_effective` → `within the bounds of buffer_effective`.
- L1497: `must not larger than` → `must not be larger than`.
- L1524: `if buffer_length exceed` → `if buffer_length exceeds`.
- L1531: `when scan backward the buffer_effective` → `when scanning buffer_effective backward`.
- L1577: `must always have valid values` → `must always have a valid value`.
- L1614: `Existing vowels have level tone` → `Existing vowels must have level tone` / `...a level tone`.
- L1724: `Reset all tone in vowels` → `Reset all tones in vowels`.
- L1784: `has been modified the buffer_effective` → `has modified buffer_effective`.
- L1792: `only add one character` → `only added one character`.
- L1811: `less than 1 character of the buffer_effective length` → awkward; likely `less than the buffer_effective length by 1 character` or `one character less than buffer_effective length`.
- L1831, L1860, L1889, L3917: `Verify every the spans, must exactly the same with the input` → `Verify every span is exactly the same as the input.`
- L1869: `just exceed` → `just exceeds`.
- L1887: `the input exceed` → `the input exceeds`.
- L1898: `adds a literally` → `adds a character literally`.
- L1917: `State.add start` → `State.add starts`.
- L1974, L2017, L2052, L2091: `State.add apply...` → `State.add applies...`.
- L2122: `State.add override...` → `State.add overrides...`.
- L2173: `State.add cancel...` → `State.add cancels...`.
- L2273: `charaters` → `characters`; `cancel existing diacritic will switch` → `cancelling an existing diacritic switches`.
- L2443, L2599: `State.add auto-fill...` → `State.add auto-fills...`.
- L2666, L2689, L2737: `State.add switch...` → `State.add switches...`.
- L2737: `append F, J ... on word` → `appending F or J ... to a word`.
- L2824: `pseudo-word has no vowel` → acceptable, but for consistency with code comments maybe `pseudoword`.
- L3336: `will scan and provide pseudoword correctly` → `scans and provides a pseudoword correctly`.
- L3435, L3454, L3473: `will reduce` → better test grammar: `reduces`.
- L3608: `when modify and append` → `when modifying and appending`.
- L3818: `when append literally` → `when appending literally`.
- L3875: `wrapper for initialize allocated memory` → `wrapper for initializing allocated memory`.
- L3895, L3993: `only specify on this test` → `specified only in this test`.
- L3970: `capacity must exactly the` → `capacity must be exactly`.
- L3990: `accept the provided allocations` → `accepts the provided allocations`.

My comment:

- L1811: use "one character less than buffer_effective length."

Apply the fixes.
